figures.caption: Figure 3: Mean fresh-cloud error reductions relative to the plain PINN baseline. Positive bars mean lower mean error than the baseline. Both schedules improve all four fresh-cloud metrics shown here.

Table A.5: Summary of the FD grid checks used to calibrate the Stage 2 wall-reference solution. Relative L2 changes are computed on common grid points after refinement. For the wall-reference slice, the most important quantity is the wall-normal flux; the corresponding radial-refinement changes are substantially smaller than the shell-versus-OFF differences reported in the main Stage 2 results.

Figure 2: Three-seed mean field-residual frontier for Stage 1. The fixed AD and fixed FD runs (squares) achieve the lowest residual RMSE, while the scheduled runs (circles) achieve the best field accuracy among the core regularized arms.

Table 1: Benchmark inheritance versus new contribution. The present manuscript reuses the PINN3D annular benchmark from earlier work, but adds a new structured residual-gradient regularizer together with a two-stage empirical study designed to separate mechanism from application.

Figure 5: Six-seed Stage-2 comparison on the two primary wall-facing metrics under the main Kourkoutas-β regime. Seeds are shown as discrete replicate categories rather than along a continuous axis. The fixed shell (squares) improves both the wall BC RMSE and the wall-flux RMSE on all six seeds.

Figure 1: Stage-2 problem geometry and auxiliary shell. Left: cross-section of the annular domain with wavy outer wall ro(θ) and the shaded body-fitted shell used by the FD regularizer. Right: structured shell bank in computational coordinates (s, θ, z). The indices (i, j, k) refer respectively to radial shell line, azimuthal node, and axial node.
Table C.10: 20k anti-grid-lock ablation on the phase-safe 64 × 64 family.

Figure 4: Mean Stage-2 reductions relative to the OFF baseline across seeds 0–5. The fixed shell improves all four metrics shown here, with the largest relative gains on the wall-flux and wall-BC quantities that motivate the shell construction.

Table 3: Core 30k three-seed Stage-1 comparison on the fresh Sobol audit. Entries are mean ± sample standard deviation over seeds 0, 1, and 2. In the scaled columns, the numbers shown are mantissas under the power of ten indicated in the header.

Table 2: Stage-2 protocol. The benchmark, architecture, and base objective are inherited from the PINN3D/Kourkoutas-β line, while the shell regularizer and the wall-audit logic are the new ingredients introduced here.
Table B.8: Completed six-seed Adam999 sweep at initial learning rate 10−3, comparing OFF and the fixed shell.
Table B.7: Completed six-seed Stage 2 sweep under the main Kourkoutas-β regime, comparing OFF and the fixed shell.

Table B.6: Seed-2 optimizer and initial-learning-rate sensitivity for Stage 2, comparing OFF and the fixed shell. The inherited schedule starts from 7.5 × 10−3 and decays to 10−5; the milder probes use the same cosine form but start from 10−3.
